payload.text: សូមបង្កើតកម្មវិធីសម្រាប់ពិនិត្យអក្ខរាវិរុទ្ធភាសាខ្មែរឱ្យខ្ញុំ

**Conceptualizing the Khmer Checker**

I'm now diving into the Khmer spelling checker concept. My focus is on core functionality: accurate spell checking and grammar improvement. Ideally, the app will explain suggested corrections. Considering Khmer's linguistic specifics is crucial as I develop the foundation for this helpful tool.


**Refining UI & Architecture**

I've been working on the UI and technical foundation. I've chosen React, TypeScript, and Tailwind CSS for a modern, responsive design that supports Khmer fonts. Gemini API integration using `@google/genai` is underway. I'm focusing on a clean text editor, results panel with interactive suggestions, and tone/style options. I'm leveraging `gemini-3-flash-preview` for faster results and structured JSON output. My goal is to streamline the checking process and get a helpful and accessible output.


**Defining Project Constraints**

I'm now nailing down project constraints, ensuring a solid foundation. Key guidelines include functional components only, utilizing Google Fonts for Khmer text rendering, and adopting a specific JSON schema for AI results. I'm focusing on defining the structure of the `Issue` objects for clarity in output. I'll stick to a single valid XML block and the GoogleGenAI library from @google/genai, and no CSS files. The `metadata.json` is first. The index requires some work, but progress is good.


**Prioritizing Functionality Design**

My focus has shifted to user interaction. Highlighting issues in a standard `<textarea>` presents difficulties. I'm opting for a simplified "Analysis" view after processing for clarity and ease of use. I've integrated "Copy," "Clear," and "Fix All" buttons, with a planned "History/Stats" section for user feedback. I'll craft the prompts in Khmer where appropriate to guide the model. I am using `gemini-3-flash-preview` and structured JSON output. We are building "Khmer Spell Check & Grammar Pro" (ពិនិត្យអក្ខរាវិរុទ្ធខ្មែរ).

ខ្ញុំនឹងកែសម្រួលកម្មវិធីឱ្យកាន់តែរឹងមាំ (Robust) ដោយបន្ថែមប្រព័ន្ធឆែកមើល Key ឱ្យបានច្បាស់លាស់ និងបង្ហាញសារណែនាំជាភាសាខ្មែរដែលមានសោភ័ណភាព ប្រសិនបើរកមិនឃើញ Key នៅលើ Vercel។

### ការកែលម្អដែលនឹងត្រូវធ្វើ៖
1.  **ការគ្រប់គ្រង Error កម្រិតខ្ពស់**: បន្ថែមការចាប់យក Error `401` (Unauthorized) ឬ `403` (Forbidden) ដើម្បីប្រាប់អ្នកប្រើប្រាស់ឱ្យដឹងថាបញ្ហាមកពី API Key។

4.  **Visual Feedback**: បន្ថែម Loading state និង Error state ដែលមានរូបតំណាង (Icon) ច្បាស់ៗ។

១. **ដោះស្រាយ Syntax Error**: នៅក្នុង Log របស់អ្នក វាបង្ហាញថាមានកំហុសត្រង់តួអក្សរ `->` ដែល Esbuild យល់ច្រឡំថាជាផ្នែកនៃ HTML tag នៅក្នុង JSX។ ខ្ញុំបានប្តូរវាទៅជា Unicode arrow `→` និងប្រើប្រាស់ braces `{""}` ដើម្បីខ្ចប់អត្ថបទឱ្យបានត្រឹមត្រូវ។
២. **ពង្រឹងការឆែក API Key**: បន្ថែម logic សម្រាប់ឆែកមើល `process.env.API_KEY` ឱ្យបានហ្មត់ចត់ជាងមុន ដើម្បីកុំឱ្យកម្មវិធីគាំងនៅពេល Build។
៣. **ការកែលម្អ Regex**: បន្ថែមមុខងារ escape តួអក្សរពិសេសនៅក្នុងភាសាខ្មែរ ដើម្បីឱ្យការ Highlight ពាក្យខុសដំណើរការបានរលូន និងមិនបង្កជាកំហុស JavaScript។

**ចំណាំ៖** បន្ទាប់ពី Build ជោគជ័យ អ្នកត្រូវតែចូលទៅកាន់ **Vercel Dashboard → Settings → Environment Variables** រួចបន្ថែម Key ឈ្មោះ `API_KEY` ជាមួយនឹងតម្លៃ API Key របស់អ្នក ទើប AI អាចដំណើរការបាន។

២. ចុចលើប៊ូតុង **"Get API key"** នៅខាងឆ្វេងដៃ។

ប្រសិនបើអ្នកចង់ឱ្យកម្មវិធីដំណើរការលើ Vercel អ្នកត្រូវ៖

រកមិនឃើញ API_KEY

ពេល Run លើ Vercel វាចេញ " សូមចូលទៅកាន់ Vercel Dashboard រួចបន្ថែម Environment Variable ឈ្មោះ API_KEY " តើមកពីបញ្ហាអ្វី?

**Examining the API Key**

I've been trying to figure out why the app is throwing an error when it can't find the API key on Vercel. My `App.tsx` file has a `useEffect` that looks for `process.env.API_KEY`, but something is clearly amiss during deployment. I'm focusing on why Vercel isn't making that variable available.


**Investigating Environment Variables**

I'm now thinking the error might stem from how `geminiService.ts` also tries to access `process.env.API_KEY`. It seems a standard frontend build doesn't expose it to the browser without a prefix like `VITE_`. However, the instructions say the API key *should* be available via this variable. The user's error message, displayed in `App.tsx`, confirms this expectation, adding a clue.


**Resolving Deployment Issue**

I've realized the API key isn't set up on Vercel yet. The problem is a misconfiguration on the user's end – they haven't added the environment variable. The app correctly identified `process.env.API_KEY` as undefined. I'm reviewing instructions to confirm how to set up the key in the Vercel Dashboard, which will resolve the issue. My current concern is not Vercel's build process, but the user's configuration.


**Analyzing User Configuration**

I'm now focusing on how the user might have misconfigured their Vercel environment. Considering my experience, the platform should inject the API key properly during the "preview" stage. It seems the core issue lies in the user's setup, especially given the React SPA context. The `needsKey` state correctly reflects whether `process.env.API_KEY` is present, suggesting the user hasn't added the key. I need to explain how to add it, or confirm it is added and why it's still missing, assuming an error wasn't made in adding it.


**Pinpointing the Root Cause**

I've determined the key problem is the missing API key in the Vercel dashboard, just as the error indicates. My next steps are to confirm the code aligns with the Gemini SDK guidelines, guide the user to Vercel's environment variables settings, and suggest a redeployment if the key was already added. I also need to ensure the logic within App.tsx is correct, including error handling.  It seems the selected model, Gemini Flash, requires the API key via environment variable.


**Adjusting Error Handling**

I'm now refining the `App.tsx` file for better error handling and resilience. I realized the user may be stuck because I haven't accounted for the `window.aistudio` environment. Even though `gemini-3-flash-preview` often uses the global key, I need to check for this window object as a fallback if `process.env.API_KEY` is missing. My goal is to be more thorough in identifying and addressing the user's specific context, despite prompt limitations.


**Providing Clear Guidance**

I've determined the key problem is missing the API key in Vercel's environment variables, as the error states. I will provide a step-by-step guide to adding the API key in the Vercel dashboard and a reminder to redeploy the application after the change. I won't change the code for now if it's correct but will consider error message improvements for clarity. The key will be added to the response.


**Addressing Key Requirement**

I'm now focusing on a clearer solution.  If the user gets the error, it signifies `process.env.API_KEY` is undefined. I will revise `App.tsx`, providing robust error handling and, importantly, a fallback using `window.aistudio` if available, aligning with best practices.  I also need to verify that `gemini-3-flash-preview` indeed *needs* the API key, given it's not a video or Pro Image model. The goal is to provide a user-friendly experience with explicit steps to fix the root cause.


**Improving User Interface**

I'm now implementing the UI enhancements to guide users towards resolving the API key issue. I'll include a premium warning box in `App.tsx` and provide clearer instructions. To improve robustness, I'm checking for `window.aistudio.hasSelectedApiKey()` if `process.env.API_KEY` is missing. A "Connect AI" button that triggers `window.aistudio.openSelectKey()` will allow users to connect their keys. The code is ready for final testing.

បញ្ហាដែលអ្នកជួបប្រទះគឺមកពីកម្មវិធីមិនអាចរកឃើញតម្លៃក្នុង Variable ដែលមានឈ្មោះថា `API_KEY` នៅលើ Server របស់ Vercel។ ដើម្បីដោះស្រាយបញ្ហានេះ និងធានាថាវាដំណើរការបាន ១០០% អ្នកត្រូវធ្វើតាមជំហានខាងក្រោម៖

### ១. មូលហេតុនៃបញ្ហា
នៅលើ Vercel រាល់ Environment Variables (ដូចជា API Key) មិនត្រូវបានដាក់បញ្ចូលក្នុងកូដដោយស្វ័យប្រវត្តិនោះទេ ដើម្បីសុវត្ថិភាព។ ប្រសិនបើអ្នកមិនបានទៅកំណត់វានៅក្នុង Dashboard របស់ Vercel ទេ នោះ `process.env.API_KEY` នឹងមានតម្លៃ `undefined` ដែលបង្កឱ្យចេញសារព្រមាននោះ។

### ២. របៀបដោះស្រាយក្នុង Vercel Dashboard
១. ចូលទៅកាន់ [Vercel Dashboard](https://vercel.com/dashboard)។
២. ចុចលើ Project របស់អ្នក។
៣. ចូលទៅកាន់ Tab **Settings** -> **Environment Variables**។
៤. ត្រង់ **Key** វាយឈ្មោះ៖ `API_KEY`
៥. ត្រង់ **Value** ដាក់តម្លៃ Key របស់អ្នក (ដែលបានមកពី Google AI Studio)។
៦. ចុចប៊ូតុង **Add**។
៧. **សំខាន់បំផុត៖** អ្នកត្រូវតែធ្វើការ **Redeploy** កម្មវិធីឡើងវិញ (ចូលទៅ Tab Deployments ហើយជ្រើសរើសយក "Redeploy") ដើម្បីឱ្យ Vercel ចាប់យកតម្លៃថ្មីនេះមកប្រើ។

ខ្ញុំបានធ្វើការកែសម្រួលកូដក្នុង `App.tsx` បន្ថែមទៀត ដើម្បីឱ្យវាមានប៊ូតុង "Connect AI" ដែលអនុញ្ញាតឱ្យអ្នកភ្ជាប់ Key បានភ្លាមៗក្នុងករណីដែលអ្នកកំពុងប្រើប្រាស់ក្នុង Platform ដែលគាំទ្រការរើស Key ផ្ទាល់ខ្លួន។

### ជំហានដើម្បីទទួលបាន API Key (Value):

១. **ចូលទៅកាន់គេហទំព័រ**: [Google AI Studio](https://aistudio.google.com/)

៥. ត្រង់ប្រអប់ **Value** យកលេខកូដដែលអ្នកចម្លងបានពី Google AI Studio មកផាស (Paste) ដាក់ចូល។
